title: Loretia setacea
taxonomy: Loretia setacea is classified within the family Restionaceae, a group of plants commonly known as restios. This family is part of the larger order Poales, which includes various monocotyledonous flowering plants. The order Poales is further grouped within the clade commelinids, a large group of flowering plants that also includes the families Poaceae and Bromeliaceae. Loretia setacea is native to southern Africa, where restios are commonly found in wetlands and along streams. The classification of Loretia setacea highlights its unique position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Loretia setacea, also known as Devil's Claw, is a perennial plant native to Africa, belonging to the family Pedaliaceae. The health benefits of Loretia setacea include its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which have been traditionally used to alleviate arthritis, muscle and joint pain, as well as digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include harpagoside, a iridoid glycoside responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, Loretia setacea is often prepared in capsule, tablet, or tincture form, and is also used as a tea or infused in oil for topical application.
